headline: Hawkish FOMC pivot collides with hot PCE week — markets trying to recover the post-Warsh selloff as rate-hike odds hit 70% by year-end.
sub: Fed Chair Warsh's debut FOMC sent shockwaves: rates held at 3.50-3.75% but 9 dot-plot members now project hikes, with 6 calling for two or more 25bp increases in 2026. Equities sold off sharply before dip-buyers stepped in, aided by lower oil prices as the Iran peace deal from June 15 begins normalizing Hormuz flows. The week ahead is dominated by Thursday's May PCE — Wells Fargo forecasts 4.1% YoY, which would be the hottest print since the Hormuz shock began and could snap the recovery rally. Q1 GDP final and key earnings from Micron and FedEx round out a data-heavy week.

events.detail: Fed's preferred gauge; WFC forecasts 4.1% YoY / +0.5% MoM — a hot print cements the rate-hike narrative
9 FOMC members project hikes; rate-hike odds now 70% by year-end — recalibrating equity risk premium
AI/HBM demand bellwether; chip boom narrative under pressure from rising cost-of-capital
Alongside PCE; stagflation read if growth disappoints while inflation stays sticky
Global trade barometer; Iran energy cost pass-through and shipping volume trends in focus
50DMA support at 7,315 holding; lower short-term high sets up downside target at 7,000 if PCE prints hot

bias: Cautious / sell-the-rip near-term. The hawkish dot plot + sticky PCE risk create a ceiling on the recovery rally. Watch 7,315 (50DMA) as the line in the sand — a hot Thursday PCE print likely breaks it, opening a path to 7,000. Only a materially softer PCE or a dovish Fed speaker flip the bias risk-on into quarter-end.
